title: Operator TUI 3D Ananta logo animation and renderer abstraction
purpose: Extend the Operator TUI splash/header concept with a lightweight 3D animation path for the Ananta symbol: rotating/zooming/perspective logo animation at startup, graceful fallback to 2D ASCII/ANSI assets, and optional future use of open-source TUI/terminal graphics libraries.

context.user_goal: Eine weitere TODO JSON, welche 3D-Animation mit reinbringt und möglichst gute Möglichkeiten bzw. existierende Open-Source-Libraries berücksichtigt.
current_project_state: Operator TUI currently uses prompt_toolkit full-screen TextArea rendering via client_surfaces/operator_tui/interactive.py.
Rendering is currently string-based through client_surfaces/operator_tui/renderer.py and render_operator_shell().
A previous TODO exists for fullscreen logo splash -> compact 8-line status header.
scripts/render_terminal_logo.py can already generate ANSI halfblock, mono, plain ASCII, ASCII-color and edge-aware ASCII logo assets from ananta.svg.
pyproject.toml already depends on prompt_toolkit and Pillow, but not on Textual/asciimatics/chafa.

library_assessment.name: Existing prompt_toolkit renderer
library_assessment.reason: Already used by Operator TUI; can animate by replacing TextArea text and invalidating the app. No new dependency needed.
library_assessment.recommended_role: MVP implementation path for deterministic pseudo-3D ASCII/ANSI animation.

library_assessment.name: Custom ASCII/ANSI 3D projection
library_assessment.reason: A small deterministic math renderer can rotate points/segments/faces and map them to ASCII/ANSI without pulling in heavy graphics stacks.

library_assessment.name: Textual
library_assessment.reason: Textual has its own animation model and widgets/layout system, but migrating from current prompt_toolkit TUI is a bigger architectural step.
library_assessment.recommended_role: Optional future renderer backend, not required for the first 3D animation implementation.

library_assessment.name: asciimatics
library_assessment.reason: Designed for terminal animations and effects; useful to evaluate, but would duplicate parts of existing Operator TUI rendering/input stack.
library_assessment.recommended_role: Spike only unless it clearly beats custom renderer.

library_assessment.name: chafa / Sixel / Kitty image protocols
library_assessment.reason: Can render real images/frames in supporting terminals. Good for high-fidelity logo animation, but support varies and fallback is mandatory.
library_assessment.recommended_role: Optional high-fidelity terminal graphics backend after ASCII/ANSI path works.

library_assessment.name: pygame / moderngl / pyglet / OpenGL
library_assessment.fit: not recommended for terminal TUI MVP
library_assessment.reason: They leave the terminal/TUI model and increase dependency/runtime complexity.
library_assessment.recommended_role: Do not use for Operator TUI startup animation.

context.design_decision: Implement the first version as deterministic pseudo-3D ASCII/ANSI animation inside the existing prompt_toolkit Operator TUI. Keep Textual/asciimatics/chafa as explicitly evaluated optional backends, not as required dependencies.

milestones.title: Frame generation, projection and ASCII/ANSI shading

tasks.title: Define 3D animation scope and non-goals
tasks.summary: Clarify that the MVP is pseudo-3D terminal animation, not a full graphics engine.
acceptance_criteria: Document states MVP uses deterministic pseudo-3D ASCII/ANSI rendering inside the existing TUI.
Document explicitly excludes pygame/OpenGL/browser canvas from the first Operator TUI implementation.
Document defines target effects: rotate, zoom, depth, snake ribbon movement, A wireframe stabilization.
Document defines fallback chain: 3D ASCII-color -> 2D ASCII-color -> mono compact header -> legacy renderer.
No new mandatory dependency is introduced by this task.

tasks.title: Create renderer backend interface for logo animation
tasks.summary: Abstract the animation backend so custom ASCII and optional library-backed renderers can coexist.
acceptance_criteria: A LogoAnimationBackend protocol/interface exists with methods like capabilities(), frame_at(t, width, height, options).
Frame output is a plain string plus metadata such as visible_width, visible_height, ansi_used and fallback_reason.
Backend interface is deterministic and does not read wall-clock time internally.
Backend errors are converted into fallback metadata rather than crashing the TUI.
Tests verify a fake backend can be used without prompt_toolkit.

tasks.title: Add animation capability detection
tasks.summary: Decide at runtime which animation mode is safe for terminal, CI and user preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Capability detection considers TTY, NO_COLOR, ANANTA_TUI_3D, ANANTA_TUI_SPLASH, terminal width/height and reduced-motion setting.
CI/non-TTY defaults to disabled or render-once-safe mode.
Color capability supports ansi_truecolor, ansi_256, mono and plain_ascii modes.
Capability result includes reason codes such as no_tty, too_small, no_color, disabled_by_env.
Tests cover at least five capability scenarios.

tasks.title: Evaluate open-source library options with documented recommendation
tasks.summary: Document why the first implementation should stay custom/prompt_toolkit and what to evaluate later.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include a comparison table for prompt_toolkit/custom renderer, Textual, asciimatics, chafa/Sixel/Kitty and OpenGL-style libraries.
Docs state current recommendation: custom ASCII/ANSI 3D renderer first, optional Textual/asciimatics/chafa spikes later.
Docs list pros/cons: dependency weight, compatibility, testability, interactive integration and fallback story.
Docs mention that Textual has built-in animation concepts but migration is larger than this MVP.
Docs mention chafa/Sixel/Kitty as optional richer terminal graphics paths requiring capability detection.

tasks.title: Create lightweight 3D geometry model for Ananta A symbol
tasks.summary: Represent the A as deterministic 3D points/segments/faces suitable for terminal projection.
acceptance_criteria: Geometry model contains vertices and line segments for a stylized A shape.
Geometry has normalized coordinates centered around origin.
Model can be scaled without losing proportions.
Model includes stable IDs for parts: left_leg, right_leg, crossbar, inner_cutout.
Tests verify vertex/segment count and bounding box.

tasks.title: Create lightweight 3D snake/ribbon model around A
tasks.summary: Represent the snake as a curve/ribbon around the A with visible head and depth movement.
acceptance_criteria: Snake path is represented as a sequence of 3D control points or sampled points.
Snake model includes head, body and tail metadata.
Snake visibly wraps around or passes behind/in front of the A in z-depth ordering.
Model supports phase offset for animated slither/rotation.
Tests verify head/tail position and z-depth changes over phase.

tasks.title: Support SVG-derived fallback frame alignment
tasks.summary: Keep the 3D approximation visually connected to the real ananta.svg logo.
acceptance_criteria: Docs or metadata define how the pseudo-3D model relates to ananta.svg parts.
Optional reference overlay mode can render a static SVG-derived ASCII frame for comparison.
No freehand rejected ASCII draft is used as geometry source.
Tests verify fallback static frame can be loaded without ANSI when NO_COLOR is set.
Manual review checklist includes A visibility, snake visibility and relationship to SVG.

tasks.title: Add configurable animation presets
tasks.summary: Allow different startup styles without changing renderer internals.
acceptance_criteria: At least three presets exist: rotate_in, snake_orbit, depth_pulse.
Preset exposes duration_ms, fps, rotation axes, scale curve and color mode.
Preset values are deterministic and serializable.
Invalid preset falls back to safe default with reason code.
Tests verify preset names and basic parameter ranges.

tasks.title: Implement 3D projection and viewport mapping
tasks.summary: Convert 3D points into terminal character coordinates with perspective/orthographic modes.
acceptance_criteria: Projection supports orthographic and perspective modes.
Projection accounts for terminal character aspect ratio.
Projected points fit inside requested width/height or return clipping metadata.
Projection handles rotation around x/y/z axes.
Tests verify deterministic coordinates for known points.

tasks.title: Implement z-buffer/depth ordering for ASCII cells
tasks.summary: Ensure snake can pass in front of/behind the A convincingly.
acceptance_criteria: Renderer tracks depth per terminal cell.
Closer snake segment overwrites A segment when z-depth requires it.
Farther snake segment is hidden behind A segment where they overlap.
Depth ordering is deterministic for equal-depth ties.
Tests cover front/behind overlap.

tasks.title: Implement ASCII character shading and edge selection for 3D frames
tasks.summary: Map depth, surface brightness and edge direction to suitable ASCII characters.
acceptance_criteria: Shading uses configurable palettes for dense, light, edge and organic/snake characters.
Edge direction can select /, \, |, -, _, ~ where useful.
Depth can influence brightness/character density.
Snake body uses more organic characters by preset, e.g. s, S, o, a, c, ~ when appropriate.
Tests verify ASCII-only output contains no Unicode and uses multiple character classes.

tasks.title: Implement ANSI color shading for 3D ASCII frames
tasks.summary: Color A and snake separately while keeping visible characters ASCII.
acceptance_criteria: ANSI-color mode uses ASCII characters plus ANSI TrueColor/256-color, no Unicode block characters.
A and snake can have separate configurable color palettes derived from existing logo colors.
NO_COLOR produces identical geometry with no ANSI escapes.
Output resets ANSI at line end and before spaces after colored runs.
Tests verify color/no-color behavior and absence of raw terminal-control leaks beyond ANSI SGR.

tasks.title: Implement frame cache and performance budget
tasks.summary: Avoid expensive frame regeneration in the interactive redraw loop.
acceptance_criteria: Frame cache keys include width, height, preset, color mode, frame index and reduced-motion mode.
Cache has bounded max entries or total bytes.
Renderer can precompute N startup frames for a preset.
Performance budget defines max startup precompute time and max frame render time.
Tests verify cache hit/miss behavior and deterministic output.

tasks.title: Integrate 3D frames into existing splash lifecycle
tasks.summary: Use 3D animation as the startup splash source when supported.
acceptance_criteria: Splash lifecycle can request 3D frame by elapsed time/frame index.
If 3D backend is disabled/unavailable, existing 2D splash is used.
If frame generation fails, normal shell with compact header still starts.
Interactive keybindings still skip splash immediately.
No real sleep is required in unit tests.

tasks.title: Add shrink-to-corner 3D transition into compact 8-line logo header
tasks.summary: Animate from fullscreen 3D logo into the 8-line pinned header from the previous splash TODO.
acceptance_criteria: Transition frames reduce logo size and move target origin toward top-left.
Final frame matches or hands off to compact 8-line header renderer.
Transition respects reduced-motion mode by jumping directly to compact header.
Transition does not overlap final status header text.
Tests verify first/middle/final frame categories.

tasks.title: Add CLI/env configuration for 3D animation
tasks.summary: Allow users and CI to enable/disable/tune 3D animation safely.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports --no-3d, --3d-preset <name>, --3d-fps <n>, --3d-duration-ms <n>.
Env vars ANANTA_TUI_3D, ANANTA_TUI_3D_PRESET, ANANTA_TUI_3D_FPS and ANANTA_TUI_3D_DURATION_MS are supported.
Invalid values fall back to safe defaults with reason code.
NO_COLOR keeps geometry but disables ANSI color.
CI/non-TTY defaults to no animation unless render-once preview explicitly requests it.

tasks.title: Add render-once preview modes for 3D frames
tasks.summary: Allow deterministic preview of 3D animation frames without full-screen interactive mode.
acceptance_criteria: `ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame 3d:0` prints first 3D frame.
`ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame 3d:mid` prints a middle frame.
`ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame 3d:last` prints final frame.
Preview respects --width, --height, --no-3d and NO_COLOR.
Preview works in non-TTY test environment.

tasks.title: Integrate status header after 3D animation handoff
tasks.summary: Ensure 3D startup effect ends in the compact header with useful status lines.
acceptance_criteria: After animation, compact 8-line logo remains top-left.
Status area next to logo includes cwd/repo path, endpoint, auth, section, mode, focus and status message.
Animation metadata/fallback reason can be shown in a status line when debug mode is enabled.
No splash artifacts remain in body area after final render.
Tests verify final shell includes both header logo and status lines.

tasks.title: Spike Textual as optional animation backend
tasks.summary: Evaluate Textual animation capabilities without migrating production TUI.
acceptance_criteria: Spike is isolated under experiments/ and not imported by production code.
README documents setup, animation feasibility and migration cost.
Comparison covers Textual animation, layout, testing and dependency impact.
No Textual dependency is added to main pyproject unless separately approved.
Recommendation is recorded: adopt later, reject, or keep as optional.

tasks.title: Spike asciimatics for terminal effects
tasks.summary: Evaluate whether asciimatics provides better terminal animation primitives than custom renderer.
acceptance_criteria: Spike stays isolated and optional.
README documents whether asciimatics can render the startup effect cleanly inside or around existing TUI.
Dependency size and input/event integration are evaluated.
Fallback strategy is documented.
No mandatory dependency is added without follow-up decision.

tasks.title: Spike chafa/Sixel/Kitty rich terminal graphics backend
tasks.summary: Evaluate real image-frame rendering for terminals that support richer graphics protocols.
acceptance_criteria: Spike can render at least one generated PNG frame in a terminal using chafa or terminal image protocol when available.
Capability detection determines when this backend is unavailable and falls back to ASCII/ANSI.
No runtime failure occurs when chafa/kitty/sixel support is missing.
Security note confirms no shell injection via file paths or terminal control sequences.
Recommendation documents whether to keep this as optional future backend.

tasks.title: Reject heavy GUI/OpenGL dependency path for Operator TUI MVP
tasks.summary: Avoid accidental drift into GUI/game-engine dependencies for a terminal startup effect.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explicitly state pygame/moderngl/pyglet/OpenGL are out of scope for Operator TUI MVP.
Reasoning includes terminal compatibility, CI testability, packaging weight and remote SSH behavior.
Future GUI/desktop visualizer is allowed only as a separate project/feature track.
No GUI dependency is added to mandatory project dependencies by this track.
Decision can be revisited only with a separate TODO/ADR.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for 3D renderer math and ASCII safety
tasks.summary: Verify renderer logic independent of interactive terminal.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover rotation/projection for known points.
Plain ASCII frames contain only 7-bit visible characters plus newline.
ANSI-color frames contain ANSI SGR escapes but no Unicode block characters.
NO_COLOR path produces no escape sequences.
Edge/depth tests verify /, \, |, - are used in appropriate cases.

tasks.title: Add lifecycle and CLI preview tests for 3D animation
tasks.summary: Prove 3D animation integrates with TUI startup safely.
acceptance_criteria: Render-once first/mid/last 3D frames exit 0.
--no-3d disables 3D and falls back to 2D splash or compact header.
ANANTA_TUI_3D=0 disables 3D in tests.
Reduced-motion mode skips animation and renders final header.
Tests do not require a real TTY or wall-clock sleep.

tasks.title: Add performance and degradation tests
tasks.summary: Keep animation fast and safe on weak terminals/SSH.
acceptance_criteria: Frame precompute can be limited to a configurable frame count.
Performance test verifies frame generation stays under a loose deterministic budget on CI-sized frames.
Tiny terminal sizes degrade to static compact header.
Very wide sizes do not allocate unbounded memory.
Failure path records fallback_reason rather than crashing.

tasks.title: Document 3D animation usage and preview commands
tasks.summary: Explain how to use, disable, preview and tune the 3D effect.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include commands for previewing 3D frames at different sizes.
Docs list flags/env vars: --no-3d, --3d-preset, --3d-fps, --3d-duration-ms, ANANTA_TUI_3D, NO_COLOR.
Docs describe fallback chain and terminal compatibility.
Docs include library evaluation summary and recommendation.
README mention stays short and does not embed huge animation frames.

tasks.title: Run final acceptance and update/archive TODO
tasks.summary: Close the track only after implementation, tests and manual visual review pass.
acceptance_criteria: Run targeted 3D renderer/lifecycle/CLI tests.
Run existing operator TUI smoke tests.
Manually preview at 90x24, 120x32 and 180x48.
Manual review confirms A, snake and 3D motion are recognizable without hurting usability.
When complete, mark tasks done and move TODO to todos/archiv or leave only remaining open tasks active.

definition_of_done: Operator TUI can display a deterministic pseudo-3D Ananta logo animation at startup when enabled.
Animation uses ASCII/ANSI terminal rendering and falls back cleanly to 2D splash/compact header/legacy shell.
A and snake are recognizable in the animation, including visible depth/front-behind behavior.
The animation is configurable and disabled by default in non-TTY/CI/reduced-motion contexts.
Render-once preview commands exist for first/mid/final frames.
No heavy GUI/OpenGL dependency is added to mandatory dependencies.
Tests cover geometry, projection, shading, color/no-color, lifecycle, CLI flags and fallback paths.
